pubmed_links: [Helbig et al. (2020) De novo variants in MPP5 cause global developmental delay and behavioral changes](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33073849/); [Stodberg et al. (2023) A case of a childhood onset developmental encephalopathy with a novel de novo truncating variant in the MPP5 gene](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36646320/)
summary: PALS1 (also known as MPP5), a MAGUK family cell polarity protein critical for cortical neurogenesis, has been linked to a neurodevelopmental syndrome: de novo heterozygous variants cause global developmental delay, behavioral changes including autistic features, language regression, and microcephaly. (emerging evidence)
